headlineSummary: HN sentiment skewed negative for Anthropic and Microsoft Copilot, while OpenAI stayed mixed-to-neutral and Google Gemini had a small positive note amid a close tie between the neutral pair [E7][E13][E1].

ranking.summary: Microsoft Copilot drew mostly negative reactions about value, pricing, and access changes, with one more mixed note about the VS Code experience; it was in a close tie with Anthropic on the negative side [E12][E13][E14][E15].

Anthropic was viewed negatively overall because of trust and policy complaints, even though one comment praised it for programming; it was in a close tie with Microsoft Copilot on the negative side [E7][E8][E9][E10][E11][E13][E16].

OpenAI sentiment was mixed and roughly balanced: several comments praised image quality and benchmark gains, while a few compared it less favorably or questioned its business strategy; it was in a close tie with Google Gemini [E1][E2][E3][E4][E5][E6][E13][E16].
Google Gemini had limited evidence, but the available comment was positive about its visual fidelity, and it was in a close tie with OpenAI [E1].

evidence.excerpt: OpenAI’s gpt-image-1.5 and Google’s NB2 have been pretty much neck and neck on my comparison site which focuses heavily on prompt adherence, with both hovering around a 70% success rate on the prompts for generative and editing capabilities. With the caveat being that Gemini has always had the edge in terms of visual fidelity.

annotations.rationale: The excerpt says OpenAI is roughly tied on prompt adherence, but not better overall because Gemini is said to lead on visual fidelity.
Gemini is explicitly said to have 'always had the edge in terms of visual fidelity,' which is favorable model-quality sentiment.

evidence.excerpt: That being said, gpt-image-1.5 was a big leap in visual quality for OpenAI and eliminated most of the classic issues of its predecessor, including things like the “piss filter.”

annotations.rationale: The commenter praises a 'big leap in visual quality' and says prior issues were eliminated.

evidence.excerpt: Results are in for the generative (text to image) capabilities: Gpt-image-2 scored 12 out of 15 on the text-to-image benchmark, edging out the previous best models by a single point.

annotations.rationale: The new image model is described as outperforming previous best models on the benchmark.

evidence.excerpt: Been using the model for a few hours now. I'm actually reall impressed with it. This is the first time i've found value in an image model for stuff I actually do. I've been using it to build powerpoint slides, and mockups. It's CRAZY good at that.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says they are 'reall impressed,' found practical value for the first time, and calls it 'CRAZY good.'

evidence.excerpt: The improvement in Chinese text rendering is remarkable and impressive! I still found some typos in the Chinese sample pic about Wuxi though. For example the 笼 in 小笼包 was written incorrectly. And the "极小中文也清晰可读" section contains even more typos although it's still legible. Still, truly amazing progress. Vastly better than any previous image generation model by a large margin.

annotations.rationale: Despite noting typos, the commenter calls the improvement 'remarkable and impressive' and 'vastly better' than prior models.

evidence.excerpt: Ran a bunch both on the .com and via the api, none of them are nearly as good as Nano Banana.

annotations.rationale: In the ChatGPT Images story context, 'none of them' refers to OpenAI image outputs and says they are not nearly as good as a competing model.

evidence.excerpt: But in practice, Anthropic still blocks parts of our system prompt, so the actual behavior today does not match what was communicated publicly.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says Anthropic's real behavior conflicts with its public guidance, criticizing policy consistency.

evidence.excerpt: Anthropic really needs to make it clear what is and is not supported with or without a subscription. Until then, it's hard to know where you stand with using their products.

annotations.rationale: This directly criticizes Anthropic for unclear support and subscription rules.

evidence.excerpt: Anthropic have proved themselves to be be unreliable when it comes to CC. Switching to other providers is the best way to go, if you want to keep your insanity.

annotations.rationale: The commenter calls Anthropic unreliable and recommends leaving for other providers.

evidence.excerpt: Anthropic is obviously the best for programming, but probably nowhere else.

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly says Anthropic is 'the best for programming,' which is favorable on coding/model quality.

evidence.excerpt: Apparently it's just an A/B test. Legit LMAO moment, speedrunning reputation destruction to your entire userbase just to test a question whose answer you can probably already guess.

annotations.rationale: In the Anthropic Claude Code plan-change story, this condemns the company's test as reputationally destructive.

evidence.excerpt: I have a GitHub Pro subscription, renewed for the 2nd year, and I just found out I can no longer use Opus with it. Opus was one of the reasons I had a subscription in the first place.

annotations.rationale: Within the GitHub Copilot plan-change story, the commenter complains about losing access to Opus, a valued feature.

evidence.excerpt: The value-add that Microsoft brings to Github Copilot is near zero compared to directly buying from Anthropic or OpenAI, where 99% of the value is being delivered from.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says Microsoft's contribution to Copilot is 'near zero,' a strong criticism of its value proposition.
Anthropic is named as one of the providers delivering '99% of the value,' which is favorable in the comparison.
OpenAI is named as one of the providers delivering '99% of the value,' which is favorable in the comparison.

evidence.excerpt: This is quite the rug pull. I've been using the Pro+ with Opus 4.6 very successfully and being charged 3x rate was mostly acceptable. But removing Opus 4.6 and replacing with Opus 4.7 with a 7x rate is just insane!

annotations.rationale: The commenter calls the plan change a 'rug pull' and the new rate 'insane,' indicating strong dissatisfaction with Copilot pricing/access changes.

evidence.excerpt: It's a shame because the VsCode copilot experience is quite good out of the box compared to all of the other harnesses I've used. But with typical lack of transparency, and sudden, harsh changes... What are they thinking?

annotations.rationale: This mixes praise for Copilot's out-of-box experience with criticism of transparency and abrupt changes.

evidence.excerpt: Are Anthropic and OpenAI rushing to IPO for immediate cash so they can delay the inevitable?

annotations.rationale: The rhetorical question suggests Anthropic may be financially pressured and acting defensively.
The rhetorical question suggests OpenAI may be financially pressured and acting defensively.
